mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching labor market peak and economic contraction, triggering risk-off repricing that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous stagnation in real GDP growth and elevated consumer price inflation compresses corporate profit margins and forces monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when stagflation occurs, or the interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production following a supply shock indicates restored manufacturing capacity and improved corporate throughput, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within a one-week trading window.
failure_condition: Industrial production rebounds while equity prices decline for 20 consecutive trading days, or the correlation between INDPRO momentum and SPX returns drops below 0.1 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts reflects collapsing consumer confidence and credit availability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as wealth effects unwind.
failure_condition: SPX rallies for 20 days following a housing starts drop below 1400, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative despite consecutive months of payroll growth exceeding 150,000, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising policy rates and persistent inflation erodes real discount rates and forces equity risk premium expansion, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets rise for 20 days while both the federal funds rate and CPI simultaneously increase, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in retail and food services sales indicates weakening consumer spending, which directly reduces corporate top-line guidance and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within a one-week window.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns following a 2-month RSAFS drop exceeding 5%, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.
